summary: An excellent audience recording captures a powerhouse Shrine show highlighted by The Other One, Comes A Time and New Minglewood.
review: Rob Bertrando’s balcony‑mic audience tape is crisp, clean and surprisingly full‑bodied – a rare gem for a mid‑70s Dead concert.  The performance itself is top‑tier: the band rides massive jams on The Other One, Comes A Time and New Minglewood, while Jerry’s lead work “floats like a bird” and Phil drops bass bombs that drive the set.  Even with a few mixed moments (a sluggish Bertha), the overall flow from an energetic first set to a soaring second‑set finale makes this one of the Shrine’s standout nights.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (13/15) are 4–5 stars, praising both the musical performance and the recording clarity; only one low‑score comment notes sound issues, so the combined show (≈4.8) and recording (≈4.5) yields a high overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Balcony‑mic capture is clear and crisp; reviewers note a slight low‑end boost may be needed but the mix is clean, balanced and better than many contemporary soundboard tapes.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is high‑energy and jam‑heavy, leading into a second set that builds beautifully with Eyes of the World into The Music Never Stopped and culminates in a monster closing jam; the Shrine’s acoustics add extra “kick” to every number.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for soaring lead guitar (“floats like a bird”), relentless wawa riffs, and an overall inspired tone.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for “dropping bombs” on The Other One and delivering a powerful bass foundation throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Described as grinning and prowling the stage during key moments (e.g., Eyes of the World), adding vocal energy.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano work is highlighted positively on Row Jimmy but called “unchanging” and overbearing on Bertha.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drumming crescendos in the second‑set transitions are praised for their intensity and tightness.
